---
title: "Notes: Orga"
format: html
---

| Time (min)  | Duration  | Topic                   |
|-------------|-----------|-------------------------|
| 00-20       | 20        | Orga                    |

::: callout-note

Mention (without bullets on the slide)
- What I expect from students
- What students can expect from me

I do not know everything. I make mistakes. ...

About you:
- Prepare an example (humor/mistake?)

    chatgpt: tell a story of myself (for the beginning of lectures)
    - short bio
    - theoretische Informatik: just reusing stanford slides (reputable instiution: good, but no own contribution)

    My Story as a teacher. Copy Stanford slides for CS vs. Evolved role...
    Also Connect to Student expectations: broad knowledge (by Heart, being able to discuss immediately instead of asking chatgpt) plus deep expertise


- Name tags?
- tools (spyder)?
- Format of the session (exercises, preparation/reading)
- Summary presentations of practitioner materials?
- Consultation hour?
- prior experience (e.g., python)?
- used spyder before? - of course, you can continue to use it.
- Ask students how they learn (PDF, ...) whether they use a knowledge management system (which one), ...

/home/gerit/ownCloud/data/teaching/teaching-hub/data/teaching-hub/archive/Diversity_guideline_first_session.pdf
-> special needs/religious holidays/...?

- Story (TI: Stanford slides, ... - both: hard skills + engagement with practice)
- I give my very best, aim to make this the best analytics course.
- It is the first round - some things may not work immediately. I will ask for feedback very often.
:::


In learning objectives: apply/select/design:
- **Strategy-only perspectives without hands-on work**
  (case-only / consulting-style discussions without implementing and evaluating analyses)


why analytics matters:
## Takeaway
Analytics is not just about data.
It changes **who wins, how decisions are made, and how organizations compete.**
